title: Design System: Stock Market Analysis Platform

deprecated-header-a.displayName: Deprecated Header Gradient — Indigo (slated for removal)

deprecated-header-b.displayName: Deprecated Header Gradient — Cyan (slated for removal)

deprecated-metric-a.displayName: Deprecated Metric Gradient — Periwinkle (slated for removal)

deprecated-metric-b.displayName: Deprecated Metric Gradient — Purple (slated for removal)

dead-insight-bg.displayName: Dead Insight Box Background (unused, delete the rule)

dead-insight-border.displayName: Dead Insight Box Border (unused side-stripe, delete the rule)

display.purpose: The single typographic override in the codebase — the '📊 Stock Market Analysis Platform' masthead only. Currently rendered as gradient-clip text; should render solid.

caption.purpose: The sub-header byline under the masthead. Needs a contrast fix against the dark canvas, not a size change.

body.purpose: Dataframe cells, banner text, insight bullets — entirely inherited from Streamlit's default UI font, no override.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Subtle lift on interactive cards/rows on hover, against the dark canvas. Not present in code yet — documented direction.

shadows.name: overlay-lift
shadows.purpose: Separation for any future modal, popover, or dropdown from the page behind it. Not present in code yet — documented direction.

components.name: Risk Score Card — Low
components.description: Risk Analysis tab. Solid teal fill (flattened from the current two-stop gradient) signaling a risk score under 35/100.
components.html:
📊 Dev's Risk Score

24/100

Risk Level: Low

Multi-factor analysis: Volatility (30%), Momentum (25%), Liquidity (20%), Technical (15%), Drawdown (10%)
components.css: .ds-risk-card { background: #11998E; color: #FAFAFA; text-align: center; border-radius: 15px; padding: 1.5rem; } .ds-risk-caption { font-size: 0.9rem; margin-top: 1rem; }

components.name: Risk Score Card — Medium
components.description: Risk Analysis tab. Solid orange fill signaling a risk score between 35 and 60.
components.html:
📊 Dev's Risk Score

49/100

Risk Level: Medium

Multi-factor analysis: Volatility (30%), Momentum (25%), Liquidity (20%), Technical (15%), Drawdown (10%)
components.css: .ds-risk-card { background: #F2994A; color: #FAFAFA; text-align: center; border-radius: 15px; padding: 1.5rem; } .ds-risk-caption { font-size: 0.9rem; margin-top: 1rem; }

components.name: Risk Score Card — High
components.description: Risk Analysis tab. Solid crimson fill signaling a risk score of 60 or above.
components.html:
📊 Dev's Risk Score

64/100

Risk Level: High

Multi-factor analysis: Volatility (30%), Momentum (25%), Liquidity (20%), Technical (15%), Drawdown (10%)
components.css: .ds-risk-card { background: #EB3349; color: #FAFAFA; text-align: center; border-radius: 15px; padding: 1.5rem; } .ds-risk-caption { font-size: 0.9rem; margin-top: 1rem; }

components.name: Metric Tile (Overview)
components.description: Overview tab price tile, one per selected stock. Documented target: flat Panel Charcoal background replacing the current deprecated purple gradient; gain/loss color stays inline on the percent-change line only.
components.css: .ds-metric-tile { background: #262730; color: #FAFAFA; text-align: center; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; margin: 0.5rem 0; } .ds-metric-tile h2 { margin: 0.25rem 0; } .ds-metric-down { color: #FF0000; } .ds-metric-up { color: #008000; }

narrative.overview: The dashboard sits on a genuinely good foundation it never earned credit for: Streamlit's dark shell (#0E1117 canvas, #262730 panels, #FAFAFA text) already reads as a TradingView-style instrument panel — dense, dark, low-glare, built for someone who watches numbers all day. That foundation is undermined by a second layer of decoration bolted on top: a gradient-clip-text masthead, a purple-to-violet metric-card gradient lifted straight from 2021 SaaS landing pages, and one dead CSS class (.insight-box, a side-stripe-bordered callout) that isn't even wired into any markup. The Quant Terminal direction means stripping that decorative layer back to the shell's own dark, flat, color-as-signal language — the same restraint a real risk desk or trading terminal enforces, because every accent color there means something specific (buy, sell, breach, warning), not 'this card is important'. This system explicitly rejects the generic-AI-SaaS-dashboard look this codebase currently has (gradient cards, gradient text headers) and consumer-trading-app playfulness (Robinhood-style rounded cuteness). Depth comes from contrast and density, not gradients; color is rationed to data that actually carries meaning — gains/losses, risk tiers, breach flags — never spent on decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black canvas, charcoal panels, off-white text — inherited from Streamlit's dark theme, currently uncommitted to the repo
Color is semantic only: red/green for direction, a three-stop ramp for risk tiers, nothing else
Flat by default; elevation reserved for transient surfaces (hover, modal, popover), never base layout
Dense, multi-metric layouts (4-5 st.metric columns, 9 tabs) read at terminal speed, not landing-page speed

rules.name: The Risk Tier Rule
rules.body: Color may only encode the five things that already have meaning in this codebase: gain/loss direction, risk tier (low/medium/high), VaR breach/no-breach, factor sign (positive/negative alpha or beta), and recommendation (buy/hold/sell). If a color isn't standing in for one of those, it's decoration and it's not allowed.

rules.name: The One Red Rule
rules.body: Signal Red (#FF4B4B, Streamlit's UI accent) and Loss Red (#FF0000, the gains/losses color) are not allowed to keep diverging by accident. The next time either is touched, converge them to a single deliberate red used for both 'UI accent' and 'negative number,' or pick a different, explicit Signal Red that isn't just whatever Streamlit shipped.

rules.name: The Inherited-Until-Earned Rule
rules.body: Don't introduce a second typeface or a new display size to 'make a tab feel special.' The system has earned exactly one display style (the masthead); any new typographic emphasis should come from Streamlit's existing st.header/st.subheader/st.metric hierarchy, not a new custom class.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: Nothing in the base layout (tab panels, metric tiles, the sidebar) carries a shadow at rest. Shadows only appear in direct response to interaction state (hover, focus) or for content that's genuinely floating above the page (overlays, popovers).

dos: Do keep the dark Canvas Black / Panel Charcoal / Ink White base — commit it explicitly via a .streamlit/config.toml [theme] block instead of relying on each viewer's inherited default, so the deployed app looks the same for every recruiter who opens it.
Do reserve color strictly for the five meaningful signals: gain/loss direction, risk tier, VaR breach, factor sign, buy/hold/sell — per the Risk Tier Rule.
Do use the native st.info / st.success / st.warning / st.error banners for any new contextual callout — they already deliver the 'instrumented and quiet' feel the system wants.
Do keep the base layout flat; introduce shadows only for hover/focus states and transient overlays, per the Flat-at-Rest Rule.
Do fix .sub-header's color: #666 — it was written against an assumed light page and fails contrast against the #0E1117 dark canvas it actually renders on.
donts: Don't use gradient-clip-text headings (-webkit-background-clip: text) anywhere — explicitly the AI-SaaS tell flagged in PRODUCT.md's anti-references. Remove .main-header's #2E3192 → #1BFFFF gradient and render it as solid Ink White.

Don't use decorative gradient card backgrounds where the gradient carries no information — explicitly the .metric-card (#667EEA → #764BA2) pattern. The only gradient/color-coded card allowed to exist is the risk score card, because its color is the data.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe on any card or callout — .insight-box's 4px #667EEA left border is dead code; delete the rule, don't ever wire it up.

Don't introduce a second display typeface or a new heading size 'for emphasis.' One display style exists (the masthead); everything else borrows Streamlit's native st.header/st.subheader/st.metric hierarchy.

Don't add a new one-off card style (a new radius, a new gradient, a new padding scale) per tab. Extend the three radius tokens (sm 5px / md 10px / lg 15px) and three spacing tokens (sm 0.5rem / md 1rem / lg 1.5rem) already in use rather than inventing a fourth value.
